Title: Titanic Survival: Crew vs 3rd Class by Sex — Historical 1912
Source: British Board of Trade (1990). Report on the Loss of the 'Titanic' (S.S.). R datasets::Titanic.
note: When comparing Crew to 3rd class passengers, 3rd class actually has a marginally higher pooled survival rate (25.2% vs 24.0%) due to the massive male skew of crew. Within each sex, crew fare better than or equal to 3rd class (male: 22.3% vs 17.3%; female: 87.0% vs 45.9%). This is an informative reversal case — a mild pooled reversal driven by sex composition, but not a strong Simpson's paradox.
